current_text: Test and evaluate precious metals with confidence using this gold testing kit designed for simple, chemical free identification. Built around a traditional touchstone testing method, this metal tester allows you to quickly compare metal samples and determine relative purity, without the need for acids or complicated procedures. ToolTreaux's precious metal tester includes 999 (silver), 10k gold, 14k gold and 18k gold testing options.To use, simply draw your item across a touchstone to create a visible mark, then select the corresponding tester point and compare the results side by side. Matching streaks indicate similar metal composition, while differences can help identify variations in karat or alloy content. This straightforward process makes it easy for both beginners and experienced users to assess gold and silver content. To further testing accuracy, use acid testing solutions that correspond to the metal being tested (not included).Crafted from durable metal with clearly stamped reference markings, this tester is designed for consistent, repeatable performance. Key Features MULTIPLE MESH SIZES: 1/2", 1/4", 1/8", 1/12", 1/20", 1/30", 1/50", 1/100" STACKABLE DESIGN: Fits on any standard 3 or 5 gallon bucket, stack multiple classifiers for efficient recovery RUGGED WEATHERPROOF DESIGN: Made from high-impact durable ABS and 304 stainless steel wire mesh, will not rust or corrode under normal wear GOLD PROSPECTING EQUIPMENT: Sift out rocks and soil from your high-yielding pay dirt before working it through a gold pan or sluice box MULTIPURPOSE SIFTING SCREENS: Ideal for many different purposes and a wide range of materials, such as gardening, archaeology, construction, metal detecting, arts and crafts, and more! Specifications MPN: ASRO8GCS UPC: 874211253964 Mesh Sizes: 1/2", 1/4", 1/8", 1/12", 1/20", 1/30", 1/50", 1/100" Wire Thickness (mm): 1.40, 0.95, 0.60, 0.45, 0.35, 0.30, 0.17, 0.14, 0.10 Material: High Impact ABS and 304 Stainless Steel Number of Pieces: 8 Type: Classifier Screen Set In short, gold is heavy, meaning it will sink to the bottom of a gold pan; but, if a gold pan is full of oversized rocks, it messes with that natural settling process. Classifying via sifting screens lets you focus on materials where gold nuggets, flakes or flour will actually show up, improving your chances of catching it. When you classify by size, you can run each size group through your gold panning or sluicing system separately. That gives you better control and increases the odds of recovering fine gold that might get lost in a rough mix.And, if you're using a sluice, high banker, or other complex gold prospecting equipment, feeding oversized material into it can cause clogs, reduce efficiency, or even damage it. The California Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment (OEHHA) administers the Proposition 65 program and publishes the listed chemicals, which includes more that 850 chemicals.
